description_long: This is a bulbous, grayish ceramic vase with a narrow neck and a rounded base, featuring a series of pronounced, organic protrusions or ridges along its sides. The surface is slightly glossy with a subtle, uneven texture, suggesting it was hand-formed rather than machine-made. The vase's neck is relatively tall and flares gently at the rim, which appears thick and slightly irregular. The decorative protrusions are asymmetrical and resemble stylized natural forms, possibly inspired by plant or animal motifs. The coloration is predominantly a muted gray with hints of earthy undertones and some reflective highlights. There are no visible painted patterns or additional color accents, giving the vase a raw, sculptural presence.
description: A gray, bulbous ceramic vase with a tall neck and organic, sculptural protrusions along its sides. The surface is glossy and textured, with an earthy, hand-crafted appearance.
description_view_0: The vase's rounded body and two sets of pronounced, wing-like protrusions on either side of the neck are clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights a single, elongated protrusion running vertically along the neck and body, with the vase's curvature and glossy finish evident.
description_view_2: The back of the vase shows the symmetrical arrangement of the protrusions and the full, rounded form of the body.
description_view_3: Another side view focusing on the elongated protrusion, showing its layered, organic shape and the vase's overall texture.
